title: Zoroastrianism Established
fact: Ancient Persian religion with monotheistic elements, founded by Zoroaster, shapes Iranian philosophy for millennia.

title: Cyrus Cylinder Issued
fact: Cyrus declares freedom of religion and repatriation rights for exiled peoples after capturing Babylon.

title: Persepolis Constructed
fact: Construction of Persepolis begins as ceremonial capital, becoming symbol of Persian imperial power.

title: Battle of Marathon
fact: Greeks defeat Persians at Marathon; first major setback for Achaemenid expansion in Europe.

title: Battle of Thermopylae
fact: Spartan defense at Thermopylae becomes legendary stand against Persian invasion despite ultimate defeat.

title: Battle of Salamis
fact: Greek naval victory at Salamis breaks Persian dominance in Mediterranean, halting European expansion.

title: Battle of Gaugamela
fact: Alexander defeats Darius III decisively, ending Achaemenid Empire and beginning Hellenistic period.

title: Jundishapur Academy of Medicine
fact: Jundishapur Academy becomes major center of learning in Sasanian Persia, preserving and advancing medical knowledge.

title: Battle of al-Qadisiyyah
fact: Arab victory over Sasanians begins Islamic conquest of Persia, fundamentally transforming Persian civilization.

title: Jabir ibn Hayyan's Alchemy
fact: Jabir ibn Hayyan advances alchemical and chemical knowledge, establishing foundations of experimental chemistry.

title: Al-Khwarizmi's Mathematical Works
fact: Al-Khwarizmi's algebra treatise introduces algorithmic methods; his name gives rise to word 'algorithm'.

title: Banu Musa Brothers' Mathematical Works
fact: Banu Musa brothers advance mathematics and mechanics; their translations preserve Greek mathematical knowledge.

title: Al-Biruni's Astronomical and Mathematical Works
fact: Al-Biruni measures Earth's circumference and advances astronomy; among greatest medieval scientists.

title: Ferdowsi Completes Shahnameh
fact: Ferdowsi completes Shahnameh, epic poem preserving Persian mythology and cultural identity after Arab conquest.

title: Avicenna's Canon of Medicine
fact: Avicenna completes Canon of Medicine, authoritative medical text used in Europe for centuries.

title: Nizamiyah School Founded
fact: Nizam al-Mulk establishes Nizamiyah, influential Islamic school advancing education and theological thought.

title: Jalali Calendar Introduced
fact: Omar Khayyam and team design the Jalali solar calendar under Malik Shah I, more accurate than the Gregorian calendar.

title: Omar Khayyam's Mathematical Works
fact: Omar Khayyam advances algebra and geometry; his poetry and mathematics represent Persian intellectual achievements.

title: Nizami Completes Khamsa
fact: Nizami writes Khamsa (Five Poems), foundational works of Persian literature and Islamic poetry.

title: Mongol Invasions Begin
fact: Genghis Khan invades Khwarazmian Empire, beginning decades of Mongol conquest and devastation in Persia.

title: Saadi Writes Golestan
fact: Saadi completes Golestan, influential Persian prose-poetry work of wisdom and moral instruction.

title: Sack of Baghdad
fact: Hulagu Khan sacks Baghdad, ending Abbasid Caliphate and shifting Islamic power eastward toward Persia.

title: Maragheh Observatory Founded
fact: Nasir al-Din al-Tusi establishes Maragheh Observatory, advancing astronomy and mathematical science.

title: Rumi Completes Masnavi
fact: Rumi completes Masnavi, spiritual masterpiece of Persian literature influencing mysticism worldwide.

title: Hafez's Poetry Gains Recognition
fact: Hafez's Divan becomes celebrated as masterpiece of Persian lyric poetry and mystical wisdom.

title: Timur's Invasions Begin
fact: Timur invades Persia, causing massive destruction but later enabling Timurid cultural renaissance.

title: Persian Miniature Painting Zenith
fact: Persian miniature painting reaches artistic peak during Safavid period, blending Persian, Islamic, and Turkic styles.

title: Twelver Shia Adopted as State Religion
titleFa: اعتماد شیعه دوازده‌امامی به‌عنوان دین رسمی
fact: Safavid Empire adopts Twelver Shia Islam as state religion, defining Persian religious identity permanently.

title: Battle of Chaldiran
fact: Selim I defeats Ismail I at Chaldiran, establishing Ottoman-Persian rivalry that shapes region for centuries.

title: Nader Shah's Invasion of Delhi
fact: Nader Shah invades Delhi, restoring Persian military might and wealth, establishing himself as major regional power.

title: Treaty of Gulistan
fact: Persia cedes territory to Russia, marking beginning of Russian imperial encroachment on Persian sovereignty.

title: Treaty of Turkmenchay
fact: Second Persian loss to Russia further reduces territory and establishes Russian economic privileges in Persia.

title: Tobacco Protest
fact: Persian clerics and merchants protest tobacco concession to British; first mass nationalist movement in Persia.

title: Constitutional Revolution
fact: Constitutional Revolution establishes parliament and limits monarchy; modernizing Persia's political system.

title: Oil Discovery in Persia
fact: Oil discovered in southwestern Persia, transforming economy and bringing Anglo-Persian Oil Company involvement.

title: Reza Khan Takes Power
titleFa: رضا خان قدرت را به دست می‌گیرد
fact: Reza Khan seizes power in coup, beginning process of military modernization and centralization.

title: 1953 Coup Against Mossadegh
fact: CIA and British intelligence orchestrate coup against nationalist PM Mossadegh, installing Mohammad Reza Shah.

title: White Revolution
fact: Mohammad Reza Shah launches White Revolution of economic and social reforms, deepening westernization.

title: 2500 Year Celebration of the Persian Empire
fact: Mohammad Reza Shah hosts lavish ceremony at Persepolis marking 2500 years of Persian monarchy; later seen as catalyst for revolution.

title: Islamic Revolution
fact: Islamic Revolution overthrows Pahlavi monarchy, establishing Islamic Republic under Ayatollah Khomeini's leadership.
